expectations.evidence: Line 356: const outPath = path.join(__dirname, 'Q4-Performance-Review.pptx'); Line 357: await pres.writeFile({ fileName: outPath }). File writes to the correct name.

expectations.text: Programmatic title slide (no HTML capture / no addImage for title)
expectations.evidence: Lines 69-151: buildTitleSlide function builds the title slide entirely with pptxgenjs primitives — addShape for accent line and decorative bars, addText for title, subtitle, label, and footer. No addImage calls on the title slide.

expectations.text: Uses gradient color triplets (3-stop gradients, not 2-stop)
expectations.evidence: Lines 186-207: createGradientBar is called with only 2 colors (gradientLeft, gradientRight) — e.g., createGradientBar(CARBON.green60, CARBON.green50, 800, 24). The function at lines 41-66 creates a 2-stop linear gradient. No 3-stop gradients are used anywhere.

No renderGradientBar function exists. The equivalent createGradientBar (lines 41-66) generates gradients using raw pixel buffers via sharp, not SVG with borderRadius. The function does not accept or handle a borderRadius parameter at all, so the specific borderRadius=0 handling requirement is not applicable.
Line 260-261: fontSize: 36, fontFace: 'IBM Plex Sans' for KPI values. While 36pt is within range, the font is 'IBM Plex Sans' (not 'Arial Black'). The assertion requires Arial Black specifically.

Grep for 'fill.type' and 'fill.*type' returns no matches. All fill properties use solid colors (e.g., fill: { color: CARBON.gray90 }). Gradients are rendered as PNG images via createGradientBar and added as images.
